title: QUIC CRT post-handshake ticket-bearing TLS update seam architecture
purpose: Describe the smallest honest post-handshake TLS seam: opaque ticket bytes surfaced after Finished on the managed client/runtime path, with no ownership, replay, or 0-RTT claim.
risks: If the ticket seam starts implying resumption ownership or replay persistence, the slice will overgrow into session-management infrastructure that the repository has not proven.
If the seam is wired into public API or supported 0-RTT behavior, the repository will overclaim capability it still does not own.
If the handshake completion boundary changes, the ticket seam will stop being a narrow post-Finished update and will need to be re-traced.
design_summary: The managed TLS bridge already owns the handshake-progress boundary through Finished. This slice adds a separate opaque post-handshake update seam that can surface ticket bytes after the Finished proof boundary without changing handshake completion, application-data readiness, or any public contract. The ticket bytes remain internal and opaque; no ticket persistence, replay, PSK, or 0-RTT behavior is introduced.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice covers only the internal ticket-bearing update seam after Finished. It does not add a packet parser, resumption cache, replay ownership, or a public API expansion, and unsupported 1-RTT ingress remains rejected rather than being repurposed into a hidden post-handshake wire path.
